rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer Of Respondent Shawny Williams To Shanda Loves Petition For Order To Inv Alida Te The Williams Family Trust Of Betty Yvonne Williams And Leroy Williams And To Remove The Appointed Trustee, Sha Wny K. Williams Under Conspiracy, Fraud, Duress, Undue Influence, Altering Of A Will Under An Affidavit Of Death And Appoint Plaintiff As New Trustee Pursuant To California Probate Code Section, 15642, 15643, 17200 California Welfare Institutions Code 15610.70 (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [PART 2 OF 2] Respondent's general demurrer to the Petition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) on statute of limitations grounds is OVERULED. Demurrer to First Cause of Action Fraud pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) on statute of limitations grounds is OVERULED. Demurrer to Second Cause of Action Duress pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) on statute of limitations grounds is OVERRULED. Demurrer to Third Cause of Action Undue Influence pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) statute of limitations grounds is OVERRULED. As to the special demurrer to the First Cause of Action Fraud, the elements of fraud must be pled specifically; general and conclusory allegations do not suffice. (Lazar v. Superior Ct. (1996) 12 Cal. 4th 631, 632.) The First Cause of Action lists the elements of fraud, but does not specifically plead facts to support each element of the fraud claim. The specific demurrer to First Cause of Action is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Any amended petition curing the defects sustained on this demurrer must be filed with the court within 20 days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order. Respondent Shawny Williams shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Respondent Shawny Williams To Shanda Loves Petition For Order To Inv Alida Te The Williams Family Trust Of Betty Yvonne Williams And Leroy Williams And To Remove The Appointed Trustee, Sha Wny K. Williams Under Conspiracy, Fraud, Duress, Undue Influence, Altering Of A Will Under An Affidavit Of Death And Appoint Plaintiff As New Trustee Pursuant To California Probate Code Section, 15642, 15643, 17200 California Welfare Institutions Code 15610.70
rulings.Rulings: LATE POSTED TENTATIVE [PART 1 OF 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Demurrer of Respondent Shawny Williams to Shanda Loves Petition for Order to Invalidate The Williams Family Trust of Betty Yvonne Williams and Leroy Williams and to Remove the Appointed Trustee, Shawny K. Williams Under Conspiracy, Fraud, Duress, Undue Influence, Altering of a Will Under an Affidavit of Death and Appoint Plaintiff as New Trustee Pursuant to California Probate Code Section, 15642, 15643, 17200 California Welfare Institutions Code 15610.70. No opposition to the demurrer was filed. Respondent's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal. 3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal. App. 4th 996, 1008, 1009.) A demurrer must demonstrate no right to any relief under the cause of action. (See, Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236.) When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication. (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.) This does not apply to allegations expressing mere conclusions of law, or allegations contradicted by the exhibits to the complaint or by matters of which judicial notice may be taken. (Vance v. Villa Park Mobilehome Estates (1995) 36 Cal. App. 4th 698, 709.) A complaint may be read as if it included matters which must or may be judicially noticed, and such matters may show the complaint fails to state a cause of action, even though its bare allegations do not disclose the defect. (Code Civil Procedure section 430.30(a); City of Atascadero v. Merrill Lynch, Pierce, Fenner & Smith, Inc. (1998) 68 Cal. App. 4th 445, 459; ABF Capital Corp. v. Berglass (2005) 130 Cal. App. 4th 825.) Four Star Electric, Inc. v. F & H Construction (1992) 7 Cal. App. 4th 1375, 1379; Ricard v. Grobstein, Goldman, Stevenson, Siegel, LeVine & Mangel (1992) 6 Cal. App. 4th 157, 160.) Per the Notice of Demurrer, Respondent demurs generally to all claims and causes of action in the Petition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e), failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action, based on the statute of limitations to contest a trust under Prob. Code section 16061.8 having run. Respondent further demurs specifically to the first cause of action for fraud on grounds of failure to plead the elements of fraud with the specificity and particularity required by California law. Neither the Petition, nor either the Memorandum of Points and Authorities and Declaration filed in support of the Petition, facially plead or address the statute of limitations or service of the required Notice of Trustee under Prob. Code section 16061.8. Respondent's Request for Judicial Notice at Exhibit I is the October 24, 2023 Declaration of Alycia Cavellini Attesting to Serving Notice of Trustee for the Williams Family Trust. Exhibit A to that declaration is (1) a copy of the Notification By Trustee [California Probate Code Section 16061.7(a)(l)], and (2) a document titled Notification of Trustee Mailing List July 23, 2021, which includes Petitioner at the address 130 Westwood Drive, San Francisco, CA. Although the court may take judicial notice of Exhibit I, including existence of the documents, "When judicial notice is taken of a document, however, the truthfulness and proper interpretation of the document are disputable." (Richtek USA, Inc. v. uPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal. App. 4th 651, 660.) [PART 1 OF 2]
